Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Problem Deconstruction: Tree Merging Reconstruction Goal: Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree T_initial into a final rooted tree T_final. 1. Algorithmic Paradigm: Constructive Approach using Post-order Traversal and Greedy Simulation. The problem asks for any valid sequence, and the structure of tree merging (combining siblings under a new parent) suggests that operations must be performed from the leaves upward to maintain tree integrity. 2. State Transformations: Initial State: A set of nodes representing T_initial with their parent-child relationships. Operation: A merge operation takes a set of siblings {s1, s2, ..., sk} and replaces them with a single node u that becomes the new child of the siblings' original parent. Target State: The structure of T_final. Transformation Logic: For every node u in T_final, identify its children in T_final. These children must have been formed by merging the children of u in T_initial (or descendants thereof). The sequence must process nodes such that a parent is only 'merged' after all its required children are formed. 3. Necessary Invariants: Hierarchy Invariant: A merge operation at node u can only occur if all nodes being merged are currently direct siblings under u in the evolving tree structure. Connectivity Invariant: At any step t, the graph must remain a valid forest/tree structure where every node has at most one parent. Completeness Invariant: The total number of operations must account for the reduction in the number of nodes from |V_initial| to |V_final|. 4. Algorithmic Roadmap: 1. Data Structures: adj_initial: Adjacency list for the starting tree. adj_final: Adjacency list for the target tree. current_children[u]: A dynamic list (or set) tracking the current children of node u during the reconstruction simulation. queue: A queue to manage nodes whose children are ready to be merged. 2. Step-by-Step Execution: Step 1 (Mapping): Map the relationship between nodes in T_initial and T_final. Note that nodes in T_final correspond to specific subtrees in T_initial. Step 2 (Bottom-Up Processing): Perform a post-order traversal (or use a topological sort based on depth) of T_final. Step 3 (Merge Identification): For each node u in T_final (from deepest to root): Identify the children of u in T_final: {c1, c2, ..., cm}. Identify the current children of u in the evolving tree (derived from T_initial):
